sections.paragraph:
ἀποστεγάζω (s. στέγη) 1 aor. ἀπεστέγασα (in the sense ‘uncover’: Strabo; IG XII/3, 325, 30 [Thera, II a.d.]; Jer 49:10 Sym.; in the sense ‘cover’: Theoph., De Causis Plantarum 5, 6, 5; Aristot., Problemata 924a, 37) unroof τ. στέγην remove the roof Mk 2:4 (so Strabo 4, 4, 6 pass. of a custom of unroofing and roofing a temple on the same day; 8, 3, 30 of a statue of Zeus: if Z. stood up he would unroof the temple; Artem. 2, 36 p. 137, 26; SIG 852, 29f στοὰ … ἀπεστέγασται μὲν ὅλη).—S. lit. on στέγη.—DELG s.v. στέγω.

aor. aor. = aorist

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Z Z = Zeitschrift

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 120
